data.title: IBM Q4 earnings preview: Software, infrastructure growth and free cash flow in focus
data.description:
IBM (IBM) is set to announce fourth-quarter earnings on Wednesday, and investors will watch out for the tech firm’s revenue gains from artificial intelligence-powered demand for its software and infrastructure units as well as free cash flow.   

Wall Street expects the company to post an EPS of $4.29, implying an increase of 9.4% year-over-year, while revenue is expected to rise 9.5% to $19.21B for the quarter.

IBM CEO Arvind Krishna, during the Q3 earnings call, raised full-year expectations with revenue growth of over 5% and free cash flow of about $14B for 2025. Krishna also forecasted continued double-digit growth in automation and mid-teens growth for Red Hat through 2026, with low single-digit growth for transaction processing.

Goldman Sachs maintained its Buy rating and $350 price target, raising full-year 2026 EPS estimates to $11.50 from $11.40.

"We expect 2026 guidance to show modest organic revenue acceleration on gradually improving customer demand, although enterprises will likely continue to rationalize discretionary IT budgets given the ongoing impact of tariffs, shifting government policies, and AI disruption," said Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider.

Stifel rated IBM a Buy with a price target of $325, implying nearly 10% upside from Monday’s close of $296.33.

The brokerage firm stated that it expects 7%-8% organic software growth in 2026 and that software acquisitions will provide organic growth tailwinds into 2027. Additionally, services fundamentals are modestly improving, and there is continued opportunity for margin expansion in 2026/2027.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings and analysts consider IBM a Hold, while Wall Street analysts rated the stock a Buy.  

IBM shares have gained nearly 32% over the past year, outperforming the 15.6% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Seeking Alpha analyst Joseph Parrish maintained a Buy rating, saying IBM trades near fair value, with potential upside if growth and AI adoption continue to outperform expectations.

Over the last two years, IBM has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have not seen any upward revisions versus six downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen three upward revisions compared with four downward revisions.

data.title: Still on top of beer sales, Anheuser-Busch is finding better growth with cocktails in a can
data.description:
While Anheuser-Busch InBev's (BUD) core beer volumes were modestly down in 2025 across both retail and wholesale channels, the Beyond Beer portfolio of spirits-based RTDs, seltzers, canned cocktails and non-alcohol beer grew at a much faster double-digit pace and is positioned as a key growth engine into 2026. 

In the U.S., the beer giant still has the top-selling beer (Michelob Ultra) and the third-best-selling beer (Bud Light) but has run up against industry-wide headwinds that include young adults preferring other options to beer at a higher rate than prior generations.

Anheuser-Busch (BUD) has played into that trend by promoting its Cutwater Spirits and NÜTRL brands, which has made the company the fastest-growing spirits supplier in the U.S.

Evercore ISI analyst Robert Ottenstein noted that with the spirits category in flux across the alcohol industry, Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) is well-positioned to continue to capitalize on the disarray through RTDs.

During a recent company event, Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) executive said Cutwater is receiving a big boost to media spending, with plans to double sales and marketing spending in comparison to a year ago. Notably, Cutwater will see the largest investment ever from a Beyond Beer brand. It was noted that "adding mass reach" and "partnerships at scale" are key for more Cutwater growth. Meanwhile, the push to take NÜTRL to more premises continues, highlighted Ottenstein.

Anheuser-Busch's (BUD) Beyond Beer portfolio includes Cutwater Spirits, Brutal Fruit, Mike's Hard Lemonade, BeatBox, Phorm Energy, and NÜTRL.

data.title: Crypto network Mesh's valuation hits $1B in latest financing round
data.description:
Mesh closed a $75M series C funding round, bringing the crypto payment network firm's valuation to $1B, the company said on Tuesday.

Dragonfly Capital led the round with participation from Paradigm, Moderne Ventures, Coinbase Ventures (COIN), SBI Investment, and Liberty City Ventures. With the latest round, Mesh has raised a total of $200M since its founding.

The company will use the proceeds to expand into regions such as Latin America, Asia, and Europe, fuel product development, and strengthen its network that already reaches more than 900M users worldwide.

Mesh aims to take a fragmented crypto landscape that creates friction in customer payments and focus on "building the necessary infrastructure now to connect wallets, chains, and assets, allowing them to function as a unified network," said Co-Founder and CEO Bam Azizi.

A portion of Mesh's $75M series C round was settled using stablecoins to demonstrate that its infrastructure is ready for real-world use, the company said.

data.title: Roper projects 8% revenue growth and $21.30–$21.55 DEPS for 2026 while accelerating AI strategy and capital deployment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Roper Technologies (ROP) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Neil Hunn, President and CEO, highlighted "solid execution in 2025," citing revenue up 12%, EBITDA up 11%, and free cash flow up 8%. Hunn noted that "enterprise software bookings grew in the low double-digit range for the year" and emphasized ongoing investments in talent, strategy, and execution. Hunn stated, "our organizational focus and resolve are even stronger coming into this year. We've upscaled talent, sharpened strategy and improved execution across the portfolio."
  • The CEO addressed AI product opportunities, explaining that Roper's businesses "sit directly inside mission-critical, high-frequency workflows where we already have deep domain knowledge, proprietary data and trusted distribution." To accelerate AI, Roper hired Shane Luke and Eddie Raffaele to lead the AI accelerator team, aiming to "deploy AI with increasing speed and market-specific precision while scaling what works."
  • On capital allocation, Hunn reported deploying $3.3 billion toward vertical software acquisitions in 2025, including CentralReach and Subsplash, and repurchasing 1.1 million shares for $500 million in Q4. He said, "we have north of $6 billion of capacity for potential M&A and share repurchases."
  • Jason Conley, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "we finished ahead of expectations on DEPS, driven by very strong margin performance." He noted revenue of $2.06 billion, up 10% over the prior year, EBITDA of $818 million, and DEPS of $5.21, which was "above our guidance range."

Outlook

  • Roper projects full-year 2026 revenue growth in the "8% area," organic revenue growth between 5% and 6%, and adjusted DEPS of $21.30 to $21.55. The guidance includes a "full year effective tax rate in the 21% area" and a Q1 DEPS range of $4.95 to $5. Management clarified, "our full year guidance does not bake in improvement at Deltek's GovCon business or in DAT's freight market and assumes modest top line weakness at Neptune versus 2025."
  • Hunn emphasized a conservative approach: "We're not starting the year assuming organic growth will inflect in 2026 despite the traction we believe we're starting to achieve."

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q4 was $2.06 billion, up 10% over the prior year, with 5% acquisition contribution and 4% organic growth. EBITDA for Q4 was $818 million, up 10% year-over-year. DEPS for Q4 was $5.21, up $0.40 over the prior year.
  • For 2025, revenue was $7.9 billion, up 12% over the prior year. EBITDA reached $3.1 billion at a 39.8% margin. DEPS was $20, up 9% over the prior year, and free cash flow was nearly $2.5 billion, up 8%.
  • Application Software segment delivered 16% revenue growth and 5% organic growth for the year, with EBITDA margins at 42.5%. Network Software revenue was up 8% and organic revenue up 4%, with EBITDA margins at 54.1%. TEP segment revenue grew 7%, organic 6%, with EBITDA margins at 35.7%.
  • Roper ended 2025 with a net leverage ratio of 2.9x, $300 million of cash, and $2.7 billion available on its revolver.

Q&A

  • Brent Thill, Jefferies: Asked about Deltek's inclusion in the 2026 guide and protections against a government shutdown. Jason Conley responded, "we are not assuming an improvement this year... we're not really assuming improvement in that market until we see it."
  • Thill followed up on Procare, to which Hunn explained, "the problem now... we're slow to implement the software, which means we're slow to implement the payments... it's a completely fixable problem."
  • Clarke Jeffries, Piper Sandler: Queried capital deployment expectations for 2026. Hunn stated, "it's hard to set an expectation that says we'd be -- we're going to get X dollars deployed against the $6 billion in M&A or buyback, but we like having both levers available to us."
  • Joseph Vruwink, Baird: Asked about quantifying AI's impact. Hunn said, "we do aspire to be able to report a number, like AI revenue SKU related... but this is actually -- it says simple, it does pretty hard from how we're going to be able to sort of report this where it's credible."
  • Dylan Becker, William Blair: Inquired about the trade-off between cloud migration and long-term economics. Conley replied, "Deltek's really put a lot of AI functionality into their cloud product... that will obviously increase customer lifetime value, but it will have a more muted impact in year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism but pressed on headwinds at Deltek, Procare, and capital deployment discipline, with a neutral to slightly negative tone in recurring questions about growth inflection and portfolio setbacks.
  • Management's sentiment was confident during prepared remarks, with phrases such as "we're very encouraged by the size and quality of our acquisition pipeline," but shifted to a more cautious and explanatory tone in Q&A, repeatedly stressing, "we are not assuming improvement" in challenged segments and emphasizing conservative guidance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remains confident but more conservative, especially around organic growth and market recoveries, while analysts' tone is increasingly focused on risk factors and execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 maintains a conservative posture, explicitly excluding improvement at Deltek GovCon and DAT. In the previous quarter, management suggested potential improvements in Deltek based on appropriations but did not commit to it in guidance.
  • Both quarters emphasized AI as a strategic growth lever, but Q4 saw the formalization of the AI accelerator team and greater detail on product commercialization.
  • Capital deployment capacity increased from $5 billion to over $6 billion, with continued focus on disciplined M&A and opportunistic buybacks.
  • Analysts' focus shifted further toward the reliability of guidance and the resolution of underperforming assets, while management highlighted lessons learned and faster corrective actions, especially in portfolio governance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified lower-than-expected organic growth in 2025, persistent headwinds in Deltek's GovCon segment due to government shutdowns, and continued freight market weakness affecting DAT.
  • Procare's underperformance was attributed to slow implementation, with management prioritizing improvements in execution and customer onboarding.
  • Neptune faces commercial challenges tied to tariff surcharging, and guidance assumes no market recovery in 2026.
  • Analysts raised concerns about guidance conservatism, delayed deal closures at Deltek, and margin sustainability, with management reiterating a cautious outlook until improvements are sustained.

Final Takeaway

Roper Technologies delivered strong financial results in 2025, driven by double-digit revenue and EBITDA growth, significant capital deployment, and advancing AI initiatives. Management is taking a disciplined and conservative approach to 2026 guidance, withholding upside from challenged segments until recovery is evident, while maintaining a robust pipeline for acquisitions and buybacks. The company is investing in talent and technology, particularly AI, to drive future growth and profitability, with leadership asserting confidence in Roper’s competitive positioning and long-term cash flow compounding strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Celestica Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Celestica (CLS) is set to report its earnings for the fourth-quarter on Wednesday, after market close.

Wall Street expects the company to post EPS of about $1.76, up 58.6% year-over-year, while the consensus revenue estimate is $3.49 billion, up 36.9% year-over-year.

The company is heading into its Q4 report following management commentary pointing to sustained data-center demand and solid partnerships with hyperscalers. It highlighted expanding AI data-center offerings and outlined expectations for continued growth driven by demand for high-bandwidth networking products.

During the quarter, Celestica launched the SD6300 ultra-dense storage system and a new family of 1.6TbE data-center switches for AI and ML workloads. It also announced the acceptance of a normal course issuer bid and held an investor and analyst day, while flagging weakness in some verticals, with elevated inventory levels in industrial end markets weighing on its Advanced Technology Solutions segment.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system, Celestica is rated Strong Buy with a score of 4.89 out of 5. The stock carries grades of A+ in momentum, A- in growth, and A in revisions, alongside a B in profitability and a D+ in valuation.

An analyst upgraded Celestica to a bullish view ahead of earnings, citing robust AI networking demand and hyperscaler capex strength, and playing down speculation around Google Cloud shifting server assembly orders, adding that they expect management to “confidently address these rumors about its top hyperscaler client” and that the company is “well positioned to benefit from another year of strong hyperscaler capex deployments.”

Over the past two years, the company has beaten EPS and revenue estimates 100% of the time.

In the past three months, EPS estimates have seen 14 upward revisions and zero downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen 12 upward revisions and zero downward moves.

data.title: OpenAI unveils Prism, a new AI workspace for scientists
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) unveiled Prism on Tuesday, a new artificial intelligence-powered workspace for scientists.

Powered by the company's GPT-5.2 model, Prism is available to anyone who has a ChatGPT personal account, the company said. It will soon be available for users with ChatGPT Business, Team, Enterprise, and Education accounts.

“Prism offers unlimited projects and collaborators in a single, cloud-based, LaTeX-native workspace,” OpenAI said on X. “GPT-5.2 works inside your project with access to paper structure, equations, references, and surrounding context—right where the work happens.”

With Prism, researchers can use the GPT-5.2 Thinking model, as well as be able to “draft and revise papers with the full document as context, including surrounding text, equations, citations, figures, and overall structure Search for and incorporate relevant literature (for example, from arXiv) in the context of the current manuscript, and revise text in light of newly identified related work Create, refactor, and reason over equations, citations, and figures, with AI that understands how those elements relate across the paper.” 

They can also turn equations or diagrams on whiteboards and put them directly into LaTeX, which will save scientists “hours of time,” OpenAI added. Additional features include the ability to collaborate with co-authors, students, and advisors in real-time and use their edits, comments, and revisions; make changes in place; and use optional voice-based editing.

“In 2025, AI changed software development forever,” OpenAI said on its website. “In 2026, we expect a comparable shift in science, as AI begins to meaningfully accelerate discovery in several ways, one of which is reducing friction in day-to-day research work. Prism is an early step toward that future.”

data.title: S&P 500 tops new record as investors stay focused on big tech earnings
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 ((SP500)) achieving a new intraday record, getting close to the 7,000 level.

Traders' optimism was derived from important tech companies reporting this week, including Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), Tesla (TSLA), and Apple (AAPL).

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was +0.8%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -1.1%.

“Performance disparities in technology (XLK) aren’t limited to geography as they’re equally prevalent across U.S. market cap ranges; it ranks as second-worst performer among large-caps YTD while it’s outperforming 3% in small-cap space; conversely, communication services (XLC) shows opposite: S&P 500 (SP500) sector leads its parent index by 1.3%, whereas the S&P SmallCap 600 (SP600) version lags small-cap benchmark by 5.7%,” said Liz Ann Sonders, chief investment strategist at Schwab Center for Financial Research.

On Monday, U.S. President Donald Trump announced he would be slapping 25% tariffs on South Korean autos, lumber, pharmaceuticals, and other goods, up from 15%.

On the other hand, a U.S.-India trade deal was confirmed on Tuesday, which would gradually cut tariffs on most imports.

In addition, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) continues reaching new highs, touching almost $5,100 on Tuesday.

“Everyone asks why gold is hitting records. Simple: central banks don’t trust their own money anymore. When the issuers run for gold, you should probably pay attention,” said Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point higher at 4.23%, while the short-end 2-year yield (US2Y) slipped 1 basis point to 3.59%. A U.S. 5-Year Note Auction tailed.

On interest rates, the Federal Reserve is expected to keep rates the same at 3.5%-3.75% during its Jan. 27-28 meeting. According to the CME FedWatch Tool, 97.2% of investors are sure of it.

In economic news, the U.S. Consumer Confidence Index declined to its lowest point in more than a decade, while the Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index ticked up—still negative—in January.

Also, the Federal Housing Finance Agency House Price Index rose higher than expected in November, while the S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Home Price Index also rose beyond consensus for that month.

data.title: ETFs in focus: UnitedHealth Group selloff ripples across health care funds
data.description:
Shares of UnitedHealth Group (UNH) plunged 20% on Tuesday after the health care giant reported an earnings miss. The sharp selloff quickly rippled through the ETF landscape, weighing on funds with meaningful exposure to the stock.

UnitedHealth is widely held across the ETF universe, with 408 ETFs owning the shares and a combined ~145M shares held within those portfolios. As a result, outsized moves in UNH can have an amplified impact on ETFs with heavier allocations to the company, particularly those concentrated in the health care space. 

Below are the 10 ETFs with the largest portfolio allocations to UNH, highlighting where the stock’s latest pullback may be felt most directly. 

No. 1: iShares U.S. Healthcare Providers ETF (IHF), 23.39% allocation 

No. 2: Roundhill UNH WeeklyPay ETF (UNHW), 17.41% allocation 

No. 3: Simplify Health Care ETF (PINK), 9.67% allocation 

No. 4: T. Rowe Price Health Care ETF (TMED), 7.46% allocation 

No. 5: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV), 5.66% allocation 

No. 6: Eagle Capital Select Equity ETF (EAGL), 5.55% allocation 

No. 7: iShares U.S. Healthcare ETF (IYH), 5.49% allocation 

No. 8: Pathfinder Focused Opportunities ETF (PFOE), 5.19% allocation 

No. 9: Liberty One Defensive Dividend Growth ETF (EASY), 5.01% allocation 

No. 10: Invesco S&P 500 Enhanced Value ETF (SPVU), 4.96% allocation 

Other health care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH).

data.title: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott directs agencies to halt new H-1B visa petitions
data.description:
Texas Governor Greg Abbott on Tuesday ordered state agencies and public universities to immediately pause new H-1B visa petitions, halting sponsorship of highly skilled foreign workers through May 31, 2027, as the state reviews its use of the program and awaits federal action.

“Evidence suggests that bad actors have exploited this program by failing to make good-faith efforts to recruit qualified U.S. workers before seeking to use foreign labor,” Abbott wrote in a letter to agency heads.

“Rather than serving its intended purpose of attracting the best and brightest individuals from around the world to our nation to fill truly specialized and unmet labor needs, the program has too often been used to fill jobs that otherwise could—and should—have been filled by Texans,” he added.

His directive follows President Donald Trump's push for major, abuse-preventing changes to the H-1B visa program, including a proposed $100K fee on new applications.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Clorox fined $14.15M for failing to report product contamination to consumer watchdog
data.description:
Clorox (CLX) has agreed to pay a $14.15M penalty to the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission ((CPSC)) for failing to immediately report a product defect with its Pine-Sol products.

In 2019, Clorox (CLX) scientists reported that storage tanks and finished Pine-Sol products may have contained Pseudomonas aeruginosa, an environmental organism found in soil and water that can cause serious infections when exposed to people with weakened immune systems or external medical devices.

Clorox subsequently took steps to mitigate the potential for bacterial contamination but did not immediately report the incident to the CPSC as required.

“Prompt reporting of safety risks saves lives, and CPSC will not hesitate to enforce the law when companies fail to meet their obligations,” said CPSC acting chairman Peter Feldman.

In addition to a $14.15M penalty, Clorox (CLX) must maintain internal controls and procedures to ensure compliance with the Consumer Product Safety Act and submit an annual report outlining its compliance program, internal controls, and audit of compliance training.
